summary: Nifty closed marginally higher on Wednesday, while broader markets remained subdued. Analysts anticipate continued caution due to a weak rupee, high crude prices, and rising US bond yields. The market sentiment remains weak, with key support and resistance levels identified for potential future movements.

Several companies like ITC, Nykaa, and IndusInd Bank are in focus due to Q4 results and news developments, including Lenskart's profit decline and Dr. Reddy's new diabetes drug launch.

title: Sebi sends show cause notice to 6 Capital Group FPIs
summary: India's market regulator Sebi has issued a show cause notice to six Capital Group FPIs for alleged lapses in trade confidentiality. Two traders reportedly shared sensitive order details with Singapore-based Rohit Salgaocar, who then allegedly passed them to market operator Ketan Parekh. This enabled a front-running network to profit from illicit gains.

title: Birla Capital to raise Rs 4,000 cr via preferential issue
summary: The fundraise includes ₹3,080 crore from promoter group entities and ₹920 crore from the International Finance Corporation. The shares will be issued at ₹356.02 apiece, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

title: Shriram Finance or Hindalco may enter Sensex in June: Nuvama
summary: Market watchers anticipate significant shifts in the BSE Sensex this June. Hindalco Industries or Shriram Finance are poised for inclusion, potentially attracting substantial passive investment. Conversely, Trent is expected to be excluded due to a dip in its free-float market capitalization. Nuvama predicts the announcement on May 22, with changes effective June 19.

title: Parle Industries hits upper circuit amid ‘Melody’ confusion
summary: Dalal Street saw a peculiar event where investors confused Parle Industries with the original Parle candy maker. A social media video led to a surge in Parle Industries' stock. This mix-up highlights a recurring investor behavior. Similar incidents have occurred with other companies, demonstrating a pattern of name-based trading errors.

title: Stock Radar: Down 27% from highs! Patanjali Foods stock showing signs of bottoming out – time to buy?
summary: Patanjali Foods stock is showing signs of recovery after a recent fall. Experts suggest short-term traders with a high risk profile can consider buying. The stock has found support and is trading above key moving averages. A target of Rs 530 is possible in the coming weeks.

title: RBI proposes rules to let lenders disable phones for loan defaults
summary: The Reserve Bank of India is proposing to allow lenders to disable certain mobile phone functions for loan defaulters, specifically for devices financed by the loan. These restrictions, effective October 2026, will be tightly regulated, excluding essential services and requiring a graduated approach. The RBI also introduced broader recovery conduct rules, including agent certification and data privacy mandates.

title: Bitcoin Grows More Dependent on Saylor’s Buying Machine
summary: For most of its history, Bitcoin’s price was driven by a sprawling cast of buyers: idealists, speculators, early adopters, and, more recently, institutional investors looking for a new portfolio hedge. Demand was fragmented and hard to predict. In 2026, it is neither.

title: Gold Steadies as Hopes of US-Iran Truce Lower Odds of Rate Hikes
summary: Gold was little changed as optimism over efforts to end the Middle East conflict eased bets on interest-rate hikes.

title: Mark Zuckerberg 'feeling weight of' mass layoffs at Meta, says ‘success isn’t a given’ in memo
summary: Mark Zuckerberg sent a formal email to his staff ahead of the layoffs, addressing it and thanking those who have been sacked for their work.

title: Microfinance sector recovers after stress cycle but caution prevails amid geopolitical, inflation risks
summary: India's microfinance institutions are cautiously recovering after two years of stress, with improved collection efficiency and asset quality. Analysts warn that while conditions are stabilizing, growth will be disciplined due to geopolitical risks and a changing lending environment.

title: Marriott bets on outbound Indian travel surge, rewires loyalty into experiences
summary: Marriott International is focusing on the Indian market as travel demand grows, especially among younger travellers. The company is transforming its loyalty programs to prioritize experiential rewards and has formed partnerships with various sectors to enhance customer engagement.

title: Tata Capital listing gain cushions Tata Sons from dividend fall blow
summary: The one-off boost comes amid growing concern within Tata Trusts, the majority shareholder in Tata Sons, over mounting losses in newer businesses that continue to consume significant funding.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX files for what could be biggest IPO in stock market history - Key takeaways
summary: SpaceX, the rocket and satellite company, discloses finances for the first time, revealing $18.7 billion in revenue, $4.9 billion in losses, and ambitions to target a $28.5 trillion addressable market.

title: US Social Security COLA forecast for 2027 rises after fresh inflation data — here’s how much more retirees could get
summary: Recent inflation data suggests a potential increase in Social Security COLA for 2027, with estimates rising to 4.2%. The Senior Citizens League also adjusted its projection to 3.9%. Inflation has significantly impacted living costs, affecting the purchasing power of benefits since 2016.

title: Federal Reserve proposes new payment accounts for fintechs: What's allowed, what's not
summary: The Federal Reserve proposed a new payment account category for fintechs and non-traditional institutions to access payment systems, aiming to enhance innovation while managing risks.

title: Jeff Bezos pitches major US tax overhaul: ‘Bottom half should pay 0%, not 3%’
summary: Jeff Bezos proposed eliminating federal income taxes for the bottom half of earners, advocating for significant tax reform without detailing implementation. He highlighted the burden on lower earners and noted the disparities in income and tax contributions between different income groups.
